outlook.headline: AI inference CPU demand is broadening across the server stack
outlook.summary: The read-through is uniformly positive and still strengthening: data-center/server CPU demand is increasingly tied to AI infrastructure, with AMD calling for >70% Q2 growth and Intel highlighting server CPU demand plus higher core counts. Beneficiaries span the ecosystem — from TSMC, Amkor, and FormFactor to ON Semiconductor, MPS, Qualcomm, and Intel — with no company flagging a supply bottleneck; the main watch item is whether the new CPU ramp sustains at this pace into the next 1-2 quarters.

drivers.label: AI infrastructure is pulling CPUs into the inference stack
drivers.detail: Data-center CPU is now increasingly part of AI infrastructure, creating incremental demand rather than just a replacement cycle.

drivers.label: Server CPU TAM is expanding rapidly
drivers.detail: AMD explicitly described the server CPU TAM as rapidly expanding and expects >70% growth in Q2, signaling near-term acceleration.

drivers.label: New data-center CPU ramp and SKU additions
drivers.detail: Amkor and ON Semiconductor cited a new data-center CPU ramp and new server CPU SKUs, pointing to broadening product demand.

drivers.label: Higher compute intensity and core-count growth
drivers.detail: Rising AI inference compute intensity and positive core-count growth are supporting incremental CPU content and demand.

drivers.label: Balanced supply keeps demand visible
drivers.detail: Most reads say supply is balanced, implying demand is the driver rather than shortage-driven upside and reducing near-term execution risk.

risks: A pause in the data-center CPU ramp would quickly temper the current broad-based bullishness.
Balanced supply could limit upside if demand acceleration outpaces execution but not capacity.
Intel/AMD product momentum needs to remain strong for the current mention trend to keep rising.
